name: Rota do Cabo Raso — circular route PR2 CSC in Parque Natural Sintra-Cascais
name_original: Rota do Cabo Raso — percurso circular PR2 CSC no Parque Natural Sintra-Cascais
region: Cascais e Estoril e Estoril e Estoril
region_intro: Jogging around Cascais e Estoril offers a diverse landscape for runners, characterized by its extensive coastline, sandy beaches, and the natural park areas of Sintra-Cascais. The region features a mix of flat, paved promenades ideal for easy runs, as well as more challenging trails through dunes and pine forests. Elevation changes are generally mild, with some routes including gentle hills rather than steep peaks. This variety provides options for different running preferences and fitness levels. Best jogging routes around Cascais e Estoril e Estoril e Estoril The most popular jogging route is Rota do Cabo Raso — circular route PR2 CSC in Parque Natural Sintra-Cascais, a 7.9 miles (12.8 km) trail that takes 1 hour 20 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the coastal landscapes of the Sintra-Cascais Natural Park. Another top favourite among local runners is Cascais Waterfront Promenade – Boca do Inferno (Hell's Mouth) loop from Cascais, a difficult 12.9 miles (20.8 km) path. This route follows the scenic waterfront, offering continuous ocean views. Local runners also love the Sistema Dunar da Cresmina — circular route in Parque Natural Sintra-Cascais, a 2.2 miles (3.6 km) trail leading through the unique dune system, often completed in about 22 minutes. Jogging around Cascais e Estoril is defined by coastal paths, natural park trails, and urban promenades. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy jogs to more challenging long-distance runs. The routes in Cascais e Estoril e Estoril e Estoril are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from more than 60 reviews. More than 9000 runners have used komoot to explore Cascais e Estoril e Estoril e Estoril's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in the Cascais and Estoril area?
A: There are over 50 dedicated running routes around Cascais and Estoril, catering to various fitness levels and preferences. You'll find everything from easy coastal jogs to more challenging trail runs through natural parks.

Q: Are there any easy running routes suitable for beginners or a relaxed jog?
A: Yes, the region offers several easy routes. For a gentle run, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1381044088/sistema-dunar-da-cresmina-circular-route-in-parque-natural-sintra-cascais" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sistema Dunar da Cresmina — circular route in Parque Natural Sintra-Cascais&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 3.6 km trail through unique dune systems. The Cascais-Estoril Promenade (Paredão de Cascais) also provides a flat, paved coastal path perfect for an easy, scenic jog.

Q: What are the best routes for a long-distance run with ocean views?
A: For a longer run with spectacular ocean views, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14705125" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascais Waterfront Promenade – Boca do Inferno (Hell's Mouth) loop from Cascais&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent choice. This challenging 20.8 km path follows the scenic waterfront. Another option is the Ciclovia do Guincho, a dedicated path stretching from Cascais Marina to Guincho Beach, offering dramatic cliff coastline vistas.

Q: Can I find circular running routes in Cascais and Estoril?
A: Absolutely. Many routes in the area are circular, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. A popular moderate circular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1381024630/rota-do-cabo-raso-circular-route-pr2-csc-in-parque-natural-sintra-cascais" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rota do Cabo Raso — circular route PR2 CSC in Parque Natural Sintra-Cascais&lt;/a&gt;, which is 12.8 km long and explores coastal landscapes.

Q: Are there any running trails that pass by interesting landmarks or natural attractions?
A: Yes, many routes offer scenic and cultural points of interest. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14705125" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascais Waterfront Promenade – Boca do Inferno (Hell's Mouth) loop from Cascais&lt;/a&gt; takes you past the dramatic sea cave of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/110232" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Boca do Inferno (Hell’s Mouth) Rock Arch&lt;/a&gt;. Within the Sintra-Cascais Natural Park, you can run towards &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/131009" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabo da Roca&lt;/a&gt;, the westernmost point of continental Europe, or explore areas near &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2667584" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sintra Historic Centre&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/205133" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of the Moors&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go jogging in Cascais and Estoril?
A: Cascais and Estoril benefit from a mild climate with over 300 days of sunshine annually, making it an excellent destination for year-round jogging. Spring and autumn offer particularly pleasant temperatures, but even in winter, the weather is often suitable for outdoor activities.

Q: Are there any family-friendly running paths in the region?
A: The Cascais-Estoril Promenade (Paredão de Cascais) is an ideal family-friendly option. It's a flat, paved coastal walkway, approximately 3 km long, suitable for all ages and abilities, and passes by beaches and cafes. The easy &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1381044088/sistema-dunar-da-cresmina-circular-route-in-parque-natural-sintra-cascais" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sistema Dunar da Cresmina&lt;/a&gt; route is also a good choice for families.

Q: Where can I find trail running experiences in natural settings?
A: The Sintra-Cascais Natural Park is your go-to for trail running. It offers routes through dunes, pine forests, and leads to dramatic coastal points. Quinta do Pisão, located within the park, provides an idyllic natural setting with dirt tracks for those who prefer running amidst greenery away from paved paths.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Cascais and Estoril?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 60 reviews. Runners often praise the diverse landscape, from extensive coastlines and sandy beaches to natural park areas, and the variety of routes catering to different abilities.

Q: Are there running routes accessible by public transport?
A: Many of the coastal routes, such as the Cascais-Estoril Promenade, are easily accessible via the local train line that runs along the coast, connecting Lisbon to Cascais. This makes it convenient to reach various starting points without a car.

Q: Can I find routes that offer a mix of urban and natural scenery?
A: Yes, the region perfectly blends urban charm with natural beauty. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40670100" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascais Waterfront Promenade – Fort of São Jorge de Oitavos loop from Cascais&lt;/a&gt; combine the lively promenade with views of historic fortifications and the open ocean. The PR 5 CSC (Ribeira das Vinhas Route) also links Cascais to the Natural Park, traversing green spaces and historical landmarks.

Q: Are there any routes with fitness equipment along the way?
A: Yes, Avenida Alcabideche in Estoril provides over three kilometers of nearly straight running paths and features fitness equipment along the way, perfect for incorporating a strength workout into your run.
tips:
A beautiful wide sandy beach, which is located directly on a dune landscape and a nature reserve.

The beach is also very popular with surfers due to the fantastic waves 💙.
The Vabo Raso Lighthouse is one of the active lighthouses in the region, located in the São Braz Fort. The Fort was built in 1894 and most recently modernized in 2003.
